title: L1 memory tier returns 'Unknown tier' error
what_failed: Writing to tier='l1' returned {"success": False, "error": "Unknown tier"}. L1 was architecturally defined but never implemented.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py memory_write() — no elif for tier='l1', fell through to catch-all error return.
why_likely: L1 (raw_wiki Supabase table) was defined in SUPABASE_SETUP.sql and the architecture doc but the memory_write implementation only handled l2 and l3.
reproduction: In root mode: ask Pi to test all memory tiers. L1 write returns Unknown tier.
expected: tier='l1' writes to raw_wiki Supabase table with correct schema (thread_id, role, content, metadata).
suggested_fix: Add elif tier == 'l1': block in memory_write() writing to raw_wiki. Add L1 read support in memory_read().
